verdict.note: Real xterm DOM at DPR 1/1.25/1.5/2: max span overflow 0px, row overflow 0px, bad boxes 0, bottom gap 0px.
Busy TUI drag held selection for 800ms; selected text remained visible, max overflow 0px, bad cell boxes 0.

A/reproduce, LIVE gateway surface (dist of main @120024f4, DPR 1.25): the served xterm bundle (SessionInterface-sJrvMHGq.js) contains ZERO cell-box patch markers and the live DOM shows 134 glyph spans with 0 explicit cell boxes — the merged invariant never reached the deployed product (npm reinstall at 02:26 recreated @xterm/xterm unpatched; the 04:44 dist build baked it in). Right-edge truncation therefore persists for the user despite the merged fix.

B/verify on the worktree stack (branch @07fad857, DPR 1.25): the served engine module carries both patch markers (predev re-asserts the installer), the live DOM shows 141/141 text spans with explicit inline-block cell-box widths, and 0 spans cross the viewport's right edge. Artifact level: npm run build's new prebuild re-patches idempotently and the fresh dist bundle contains the cell-box marker — the silent-unpatched-dist pipeline hole (A) is closed.

Re-measure @69d309c4 on the patched selection predicate (plain drag now always selects locally): drag across a live CJK-heavy terminal — 12 rows' glyph span geometry byte-identical before vs during selection (overlay paints, glyphs never reflow), 2 selection divs rendered.

Re-measure @69d309c4 (patch script re-staled by the selection-patch addition; wide-glyph entries unchanged): live claude pane, cell width 6.62px — 40/40 CJK spans carry the patch's explicit width and land on integral cell multiples (wide=2 cells) within 2%.
